title: Klatz curry
description: This stew was inspired by the “golden poularde” dish from Umami in Berlin but significantly simplified for easy cooking at home. It has the main ingredients of the original dish: chicken, pumpkins, potatoes, carrots, rice, and coconut milk curry. The main difference is the chicken: instead of crispy breaded chicken breast we use braised chicken legs so it can be cooked in a single pot, we dropped the sweet potatoes because we’re not fans, and we dropped the salad because it’s too much trouble to make them on a regular work day. This is a perfect autumn recipe!
ingredients:
- 4 chicken legs
- 1 kg potatoes
- 4 carrots
- 1 butternut squash
- 1 onion
- 1 fresh chile
- 4 cloves garlic
- 3 slices fresh ginger
- 1 can coconut milk
- 2 whole cardamom pods
- 1 teaspoon curry powder
- salt 
- olive oil
- cooked rice
steps:
1. Prepare the vegetables:
  - **Onion:** peel, halve, and cut it into 1/2 cm slices. **Garlic**: smash, peel, and roughly chop. **Chile:** halve, remove the seeds, and thinly slice. **Ginger:** peel and slice into 1/4 cm slices.
  - **Potatoes and carrots:** peel if needed, and cut into large chunks. **Pumpkin:** peel, remove the seeds, and cut into medium-large chunks.
2. Rinse the chicken in cold water. Separate the thighs from drumsticks.
3. Cover the bottom of the Dutch oven with olive oil, and heat it on medium-high heat.
4. Place the chicken skin side down in the Dutch oven, sprinkle with salt, and cook until the skin is brown and crispy, for 10–15 minutes. Turn and cook for about 10 more minutes.
5. Add onion, garlic, chile, and ginger, stir. Cook for 2–3 minutes, until the onion is transparent.
6. Add the coconut milk, add 2 whole cardamom pods, 1 teaspoon curry powder, stir thoroughly, and cook covered for 20 minutes.
7. Add the rest of the vegetables, add enough boiling water to cover about 2/3 of the height of the vegetables, and cook covered until the vegetables are very soft.
8. Add more salt if necessary.
9. Serve with a portion of rice on the side.
